name: La Palma del Condado - Villalba del Alcor – circular route
name_original: La Palma del Condado - Villalba del Alcor – ruta circular
region_intro: Hiking around La Palma Del Condado, located in the Huelva province of Andalusia, Spain, offers routes through agricultural landscapes and distinctive natural features. The region is characterized by extensive olive groves and vineyards, contributing to the "Condado de Huelva" wine denomination. A notable feature is the Río Tinto, known for its unique red and brown hues resulting from mineral weathering. The landscape also includes the Corumbel Reservoir, providing additional natural areas for exploration. Best hiking trails around La Palma Del Condado The most popular hiking route is Villarrasa - Gadea Bridge over the Tinto River, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the distinctive red waters of the Tinto River. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Palma del Condado - Villalba del Alcor – circular route, a difficult 12.5 miles (20.1 km) path. This route traverses agricultural lands and offers views of the surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Pantano del Corumbel from Villalba del Alcor, a 11.9 miles (19.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain to the Corumbel Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around La Palma Del Condado is defined by agricultural lands, the unique Río Tinto landscape, and the Corumbel Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 16 routes available. The routes in La Palma Del Condado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore La Palma Del Condado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Palma Del Condado, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes around La Palma Del Condado. These include 7 easy trails, 8 moderate routes, and 1 difficult option, providing choices for various fitness levels.

Q: What unique natural features can I expect to see while hiking in La Palma Del Condado?
A: The region is most famous for the extraordinary &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984238127/villarrasa-gadea-bridge-over-the-tinto-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt;, known for its striking red and brown waters due to mineral weathering, often compared to a Martian landscape. You'll also encounter extensive agricultural lands with olive groves and vineyards, and the scenic Corumbel Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, La Palma Del Condado offers several circular routes. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984207806/la-palma-del-condado-villalba-del-alcor-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palma del Condado - Villalba del Alcor – circular route&lt;/a&gt;. For moderate walks, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14585102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza del Rocío loop from La Palma del Condado&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17220484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murals in Parque Villa Luisa – Plaza del Altozano loop from La Palma del Condado&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I hike to the Corumbel Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984456289/pantano-del-corumbel-from-villalba-del-alcor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano del Corumbel from Villalba del Alcor&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that leads through varied terrain directly to the Corumbel Reservoir, offering beautiful views and recreational opportunities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in La Palma Del Condado?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique landscapes, especially the distinctive colors of the Río Tinto, and the variety of routes that traverse agricultural lands and natural features.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see near the hiking trails?
A: While the trails focus on natural beauty, the nearby town of Niebla offers significant historical sites. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Niebla Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/389426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar or Castle of the Guzmáns&lt;/a&gt;, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/386978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/389433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Walls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there a Vía Verde (Greenway) in the region, and what is it like for hiking?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde follows an abandoned railway line connecting La Palma Del Condado with Berrocal, passing through the stunning landscapes of the Río Tinto. Parts of this trail can be challenging, featuring tunnels that may require a flashlight and bridges where caution is advised, but it offers an immersive experience of the river's vibrant colors.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Palma Del Condado?
A: Given its location in Andalusia, Spain, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. Summers can be very hot, while winters are mild but can be rainy.

Q: Are the hiking trails in La Palma Del Condado suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, with 7 easy routes available, there are options suitable for families. These trails often traverse agricultural lands and offer gentle gradients, making them enjoyable for younger hikers. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in La Palma Del Condado?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in the region. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them. Specific rules might apply in certain protected areas, so check local signage.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails around La Palma Del Condado?
A: The terrain is quite varied. You'll find paths winding through extensive agricultural lands, particularly olive groves and vineyards. Other routes explore the unique, mineral-rich landscapes around the Río Tinto, and some lead to the Corumbel Reservoir, offering a mix of natural and cultivated scenery.
tips: One of the most beautiful in the province of Huelva, the Plaza de España in La Palma del Condado is the social center of this picturesque town. Surrounded by unique buildings such as the Casa de los Arcos, the Town Hall and the Church of San Juan Bautista, this beautiful place is chosen to have a good time at sunset on summer nights.
It is a church registered in an old Almohad fortress that was declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1931. It is undoubtedly the most outstanding monument in Villalba del Alcor.

waypoints.name: Plaza de España - La Palma del Condado
